skill.name: branch
skill.description: Creates spec/* and impl/* Git branches for a specification-driven development workflow after checking context and asking for user confirmation.
skill.summary: Create Git branches for specification and implementation phases.
skill.author: Clionegohan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks and weak-crypto substrings, but most are false positives from examples, branch-name placeholders, and table text. The confirmed risk is that the skill instructs the agent to run Git commands that can fetch from a remote and change the current repository branch.

medium_findings.title: Git Commands Can Modify Repository State
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Git command execution, including branch inspection, remote fetch, and branch creation. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but it can change the user's active branch and contact the configured origin remote.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command block explicitly lists git branch, git fetch, and git checkout commands. The behavior is legitimate for a branch helper, but it has direct filesystem and network effects through Git.

low_findings.title: Destructive Error-Handling Option Is Mentioned
low_findings.description: The skill lists discarding uncommitted changes as an option when changes exist, although it marks the option as not recommended. This could lead to data loss if an agent or user follows it without sufficient confirmation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text clearly presents discarding changes as an available response. It does not provide a destructive command and labels the option as not recommended, so the risk is limited.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography detections correspond to ordinary documentation text such as description fields and branch naming examples, not cryptographic code or calls to weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain frontmatter, branch pattern examples, and a parameter table. No cryptographic operation, library, key handling, or hash function appears in those locations.

low_findings.title: Most Backtick Command Detections Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Many static external-command detections are inline command names, branch examples, diagrams, or conversation examples. They are not Ruby shell backtick execution or executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown examples and prose. There is no Ruby code, no shell interpolation, and no executable file beyond the instructions documented in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Git Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent to run Git commands, including git fetch origin main and git checkout -b. These commands can contact a remote repository and alter local repository state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present in a bash-labeled block and match the stated workflow. The pattern is intentional, but users should understand the side effects before publication.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose consistency when branch names differ between specification and implementation work. This skill creates structured spec/* and impl/* branch names and asks for confirmation before changing branches.
actual_capabilities: Creates branch names using spec/* and impl/* prefixes.
Uses an action ID and short description in each branch name.
Checks the current Git branch before branch creation.
Fetches the main branch from the origin remote before creating work branches.
Asks the user to confirm branch creation before running the checkout command.
Provides recovery choices when a branch already exists or local changes are present.
limitations: It assumes the repository uses main as the base branch.
It depends on Git being installed and available in the workspace.
It does not define validation rules for malformed action IDs.
It can change local repository state when the user confirms branch creation.

use_cases.title: Start Specification Work
use_cases.description: Create a spec branch before writing requirements or design files for a new action.

use_cases.title: Begin Implementation Work
use_cases.description: Create an implementation branch that maps back to an approved action ID.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Branch Names
use_cases.description: Keep branch names consistent across a team using shared spec and implementation prefixes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Spec Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the branch skill to create a spec branch for action 001-01-01 with the short description user-auth.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Implementation Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the branch skill to create an impl branch for action 001-01-01 with the short description user-auth.

prompt_templates.title: Handle an Existing Branch
prompt_templates.scenario: A generated branch name already exists.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the branch skill for action 002-03-01 payment-flow, and show my options if that branch already exists.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Safe Branch Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Local changes may exist before branch creation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the branch skill to create an implementation branch for action 004-02-02 search-index. Check for local changes and ask before changing branches.

output_examples.input: Create a spec branch for action 001-01-01 and user-auth.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes the branch name spec/001-01-01-user-auth, shows the current base branch, and asks for confirmation before creating it.

output_examples.input: Create an implementation branch when local changes exist.
output_examples.output: The assistant warns that uncommitted changes are present and offers safe next steps before creating the requested branch.

output_examples.input: Create a branch whose name already exists.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports that the branch already exists and offers to switch to it or create a clearly named variant.

best_practices: Review the proposed branch name before confirming branch creation.
Commit or stash local changes before switching branches.
Keep action IDs stable so branches map cleanly to specification records.
anti_patterns: Using this skill outside a Git repository.
Creating branches from a stale or unintended base branch.
Discarding uncommitted changes only to create a new branch.

faq.question: Does this skill create branches automatically?
faq.answer: It is designed to ask for user confirmation before creating a branch.

faq.question: Which branch prefixes does it use?
faq.answer: It uses spec/* for specification work and impl/* for implementation work.

faq.question: Does it require Git?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow depends on Git commands in the current repository.

faq.question: Can it work with a base branch other than main?
faq.answer: The documented workflow assumes main as the base branch.

faq.question: Is it safe for repositories with uncommitted changes?
faq.answer: It can be used safely when changes are committed or stashed before branch switching.

faq.question: Why is the security risk rated medium?
faq.answer: The skill performs legitimate Git operations, but those operations can fetch from a remote and change repository state.
